codeReview.summary: The fix commit directly resolves the prior blocking atomic-cleanup defect: init now pre-registers all expected artifact paths before any creation calls and always cleans that pre-registered set on failure. Compared to the original implementation (fd64e8c), which registered artifacts only after each creation call returned, this closes the mid-step partial-write gap called out in the prior review. Regression coverage was added for failures after identity, DB key, and DB creation, including retryability and non-initialized-state assertions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mors-worker skill requires manual CLI sanity checks with captured observations, but this worker run did only automated checks and still marked followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill procedure step 6 in .factory/skills/mors-worker/SKILL.md requires manual CLI sanity checks; the handoff for worker session 05b1530e-59ba-413c-9a06-4e7ec2f5056f contains only verification.commandsRun and no interactive/manual check evidence, while handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true.

summary: Reviewed feature core-fix-init-atomic-cleanup-registration as a fix rerun against prior failure in core-init-identity-and-atomic-bootstrap. Evidence reviewed: mission feature metadata, fix handoff and transcript skeleton, prior failing review, fix commit diff (9918a72), and original implementation commit (fd64e8c). The fix adequately addresses the original blocking issue; review status is pass.
